verdict_reasoning: TGF-β1 is secreted as a latent complex where LAP remains disulfide-bonded to the mature dimer, and the entire latent complex is covalently tethered to cell-surface GARP (LRRC32) or LRRC33 on Tregs, platelets, and myeloid cells via disulfide bonds. This wash-resistant covalent anchoring to a TM partner places the TGF-β1/LAP body on the outer leaflet. Clinically targeted on-surface by anti-GARP:TGF-β1 and latent-complex antibodies.
